title: “Parte3_extracao_modelo1” author: “Pedro Fontão” date: “2026-04-30” output: html_document: toc: true toc_depth: 3 theme: flatly —

#Trabalho pratico de Extração de Conhecimento de Dados Biológicos - Fase 3

Trabalho realizado por:

Alice Picas PG59750, Eduarda Santos PG58815, Pedro Fontão PG59429, Pedro Vieira PG59764

Instalação e Carregamento de Packages

# Instalar (correr apenas uma vez)
#install.packages(c("randomForest", "caret", "pROC", "ggplot2", "RColorBrewer"))

library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(glmnet)
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)

Carregar Dados Pré-processados

logcounts <- read.csv("logcounts_normalized.csv", row.names = 1)
clinical  <- read.csv("clinical_processed.csv",   row.names = 1)

cat("Dimensoes logcounts:", dim(logcounts), "\n")
## Dimensoes logcounts: 33913 300

Preparação dos Dados para Classificação

# Transpor: amostras nas linhas, genes nas colunas
exp_t <- as.data.frame(t(logcounts))

# Extrair tipo de amostra (tumor vs normal) a partir dos nomes das amostras
# No TCGA: codigo de amostra com -01 = tumor primario, -11 = normal
sample_codes <- substr(rownames(exp_t), 14, 15)
labels       <- ifelse(sample_codes == "11", "Normal", "Tumor")
labels       <- factor(labels, levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))

cat("Distribuicao das classes:\n")
## Distribuicao das classes:
print(table(labels))
## labels
## Normal  Tumor 
##     29    271
# Usar apenas top 500 genes mais variaveis (reduz custo computacional)
var_genes <- apply(exp_t, 2, var)
top500    <- names(sort(var_genes, decreasing = TRUE))[1:500]
exp_top   <- exp_t[, top500]

# Adicionar variavel resposta
exp_top$label <- labels

cat("\nDimensoes finais do dataset:", dim(exp_top), "\n")
## 
## Dimensoes finais do dataset: 300 501

Divisão Treino / Teste (70% / 30%)

set.seed(42)
train_idx <- createDataPartition(exp_top$label, p = 0.70, list = FALSE)
train_data <- exp_top[train_idx, ]
test_data  <- exp_top[-train_idx, ]

cat("Amostras de treino:", nrow(train_data), "\n")
## Amostras de treino: 211
cat("Amostras de teste: ", nrow(test_data),  "\n")
## Amostras de teste:  89
cat("\nDistribuicao treino:\n")
## 
## Distribuicao treino:
print(table(train_data$label))
## 
## Normal  Tumor 
##     21    190
cat("\nDistribuicao teste:\n")
## 
## Distribuicao teste:
print(table(test_data$label))
## 
## Normal  Tumor 
##      8     81

Treino do Modelo Random Forest

set.seed(42)

# Treinar Random Forest
# ntree = 500 arvores; importance = TRUE para calcular importancia dos genes
rf_model <- randomForest(
  label    ~ .,
  data      = train_data,
  ntree     = 500,
  mtry      = floor(sqrt(500)),  # nº de genes testados em cada split
  importance = TRUE,
  do.trace  = FALSE
)

print(rf_model)
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = label ~ ., data = train_data, ntree = 500,      mtry = floor(sqrt(500)), importance = TRUE, do.trace = FALSE) 
##                Type of random forest: classification
##                      Number of trees: 500
## No. of variables tried at each split: 22
## 
##         OOB estimate of  error rate: 1.9%
## Confusion matrix:
##        Normal Tumor class.error
## Normal     18     3 0.142857143
## Tumor       1   189 0.005263158
# Erro OOB (out-of-bag) por numero de arvores
plot(rf_model,
     main = "Erro OOB por Numero de Arvores",
     col  = c("black", "red", "blue"))
legend("topright",
       legend = c("OOB", "Normal", "Tumor"),
       col    = c("black", "red", "blue"),
       lty    = 1)

Avaliação do Modelo Random Forest no Conjunto de Teste

# Predicoes
rf_pred_class <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "class")
rf_pred_prob  <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")[, "Tumor"]

# Matriz de confusao
rf_cm <- confusionMatrix(rf_pred_class, test_data$label, positive = "Tumor")
print(rf_cm)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction Normal Tumor
##     Normal      8     0
##     Tumor       0    81
##                                      
##                Accuracy : 1          
##                  95% CI : (0.9594, 1)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9101     
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.0002288  
##                                      
##                   Kappa : 1          
##                                      
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : NA         
##                                      
##             Sensitivity : 1.0000     
##             Specificity : 1.0000     
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.0000     
##          Neg Pred Value : 1.0000     
##              Prevalence : 0.9101     
##          Detection Rate : 0.9101     
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.9101     
##       Balanced Accuracy : 1.0000     
##                                      
##        'Positive' Class : Tumor      
## 
# Extrair metricas principais
rf_accuracy  <- rf_cm$overall["Accuracy"]
rf_precision <- rf_cm$byClass["Precision"]
rf_recall    <- rf_cm$byClass["Recall"]         # = Sensitivity
rf_f1        <- rf_cm$byClass["F1"]
rf_specificity <- rf_cm$byClass["Specificity"]

cat("\n=== METRICAS DE DESEMPENHO - RANDOM FOREST ===\n")
## 
## === METRICAS DE DESEMPENHO - RANDOM FOREST ===
cat("Accuracy:   ", round(rf_accuracy,    4), "\n")
## Accuracy:    1
cat("Precision:  ", round(rf_precision,   4), "\n")
## Precision:   1
cat("Recall:     ", round(rf_recall,      4), "\n")
## Recall:      1
cat("F1-Score:   ", round(rf_f1,          4), "\n")
## F1-Score:    1
cat("Specificity:", round(rf_specificity, 4), "\n")
## Specificity: 1

Curva ROC e AUC

rf_roc_obj <- roc(test_data$label, rf_pred_prob,
               levels  = c("Normal", "Tumor"),
               direction = "<")

rf_auc_val <- auc(rf_roc_obj)
cat("AUC:", round(rf_auc_val, 4), "\n")
## AUC: 1
# Grafico ROC
plot(rf_roc_obj,
     col  = "steelblue", lwd = 2,
     main = paste0("Curva ROC - Random Forest (AUC = ", round(rf_auc_val, 3), ")"),
     print.auc = TRUE)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey50")

Importância dos Genes

# Extrair importancia dos genes
rf_imp <- importance(rf_model)
rf_imp_df <- data.frame(
  gene          = rownames(rf_imp),
  MeanDecreaseAccuracy = rf_imp[, "MeanDecreaseAccuracy"],
  MeanDecreaseGini     = rf_imp[, "MeanDecreaseGini"]
)

# Ordenar por MeanDecreaseAccuracy
rf_imp_df <- rf_imp_df[order(rf_imp_df$MeanDecreaseAccuracy, decreasing = TRUE), ]

cat("Top 20 genes mais importantes:\n")
## Top 20 genes mais importantes:
print(head(rf_imp_df, 20))
##                                  gene MeanDecreaseAccuracy MeanDecreaseGini
## ENSG00000123500.10 ENSG00000123500.10             7.593603        2.0281087
## ENSG00000161649.13 ENSG00000161649.13             5.665093        1.5179947
## ENSG00000168079.17 ENSG00000168079.17             5.427312        1.2402149
## ENSG00000101938.15 ENSG00000101938.15             5.092295        1.2812790
## ENSG00000065618.21 ENSG00000065618.21             5.041685        1.1556141
## ENSG00000115593.15 ENSG00000115593.15             4.949631        1.0287101
## ENSG00000228971.3   ENSG00000228971.3             4.883818        0.9919344
## ENSG00000149124.11 ENSG00000149124.11             4.820352        0.9003641
## ENSG00000137745.12 ENSG00000137745.12             4.712435        0.9792896
## ENSG00000174697.5   ENSG00000174697.5             4.606904        0.7967127
## ENSG00000166819.12 ENSG00000166819.12             4.605307        0.5748960
## ENSG00000152578.13 ENSG00000152578.13             4.508119        0.9388970
## ENSG00000167434.10 ENSG00000167434.10             4.331899        0.6573920
## ENSG00000104332.12 ENSG00000104332.12             4.224504        0.5990831
## ENSG00000196611.5   ENSG00000196611.5             4.112374        0.8317014
## ENSG00000181092.10 ENSG00000181092.10             4.070017        0.7201853
## ENSG00000060718.22 ENSG00000060718.22             4.028871        0.9844774
## ENSG00000181234.9   ENSG00000181234.9             3.949256        0.5436307
## ENSG00000187288.12 ENSG00000187288.12             3.867640        0.4651653
## ENSG00000185149.6   ENSG00000185149.6             3.674817        0.4291496
# Grafico top 20 genes - MeanDecreaseAccuracy
rf_top20_imp <- head(rf_imp_df, 20)

ggplot(rf_top20_imp,
       aes(x = reorder(gene, MeanDecreaseAccuracy),
           y = MeanDecreaseAccuracy)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Top 20 Genes Mais Importantes - Random Forest",
       x     = "Gene",
       y     = "Mean Decrease Accuracy")

# Grafico varImpPlot nativo do randomForest
varImpPlot(rf_model,
           n.var = 20,
           main  = "Importancia dos Genes - Top 20",
           col   = "steelblue")

set.seed(42)

# Configurar CV com caret
ctrl <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 5,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
  savePredictions = "final"
)

# Treinar com CV (usar menos arvores para ser mais rapido)
rf_cv <- train(
  label ~ .,
  data      = exp_top,
  method    = "rf",
  metric    = "ROC",
  trControl = ctrl,
  ntree     = 200,
  tuneGrid  = data.frame(mtry = floor(sqrt(500)))
)

print(rf_cv)
## Random Forest 
## 
## 300 samples
## 500 predictors
##   2 classes: 'Normal', 'Tumor' 
## 
## No pre-processing
## Resampling: Cross-Validated (5 fold) 
## Summary of sample sizes: 240, 239, 240, 241, 240 
## Resampling results:
## 
##   ROC        Sens       Spec
##   0.9987654  0.9333333  1   
## 
## Tuning parameter 'mtry' was held constant at a value of 22
cat("\nAUC media (5-fold CV):", round(rf_cv$results$ROC, 4), "\n")
## 
## AUC media (5-fold CV): 0.9988
cat("Sensibilidade media:  ", round(rf_cv$results$Sens, 4), "\n")
## Sensibilidade media:   0.9333
cat("Especificidade media: ", round(rf_cv$results$Spec, 4), "\n")
## Especificidade media:  1

Guardar Resultados para Comparação com Modelo 2

# Guardar metricas para o colega poder comparar com o Modelo 2
metricas_rf <- data.frame(
  Modelo      = "Random Forest",
  Accuracy    = round(as.numeric(rf_accuracy),    4),
  Precision   = round(as.numeric(rf_precision),   4),
  Recall      = round(as.numeric(rf_recall),      4),
  F1          = round(as.numeric(rf_f1),          4),
  Specificity = round(as.numeric(rf_specificity), 4),
  AUC         = round(as.numeric(rf_auc_val),     4),
  AUC_CV      = round(rf_cv$results$ROC,       4)
)

write.csv(metricas_rf,        file = "metricas_modelo1_rf.csv",       row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(rf_imp_df,             file = "importancia_genes_rf.csv",      row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(as.data.frame(rf_pred_prob), file = "predicoes_rf.csv",        row.names = TRUE)

cat("Ficheiros exportados com sucesso.\n")
## Ficheiros exportados com sucesso.
print(metricas_rf)
##          Modelo Accuracy Precision Recall F1 Specificity AUC AUC_CV
## 1 Random Forest        1         1      1  1           1   1 0.9988

Treino do Modelo Regressão Logistica

#treinar o modelo de regressão logistica
rl_model <- glm(
  label ~ .,
  data   = train_data,
  family = binomial,
  control = list(maxit = 100)
)

summary(rl_model)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = label ~ ., family = binomial, data = train_data, 
##     control = list(maxit = 100))
## 
## Coefficients: (290 not defined because of singularities)
##                      Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept)         1.102e+01  1.944e+07       0        1

## ENSG00000253802.2   5.993e-01  1.892e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000268104.3   4.267e-01  4.194e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000114248.10 -3.614e+00  1.218e+06       0        1
## ENSG00000141668.10 -5.291e+00  3.112e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000169550.14 -3.938e-02  1.507e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000287900.1   1.836e+01  1.081e+06       0        1
## ENSG00000186832.9   1.467e+01  8.632e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000174469.23 -5.445e+00  5.910e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000167588.13 -1.418e+00  1.566e+06       0        1
## ENSG00000255974.8   4.655e+00  7.270e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000022556.16 -2.760e+00  3.209e+05       0        1
## ENSG00000186910.4   6.779e+00  4.741e+05       0        1

Avaliação do Modelo Regressão Logistica no Conjunto de Teste

# Predicoes
rl_pred_prob <- predict(rl_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
rl_pred_class <- ifelse(rl_pred_prob > 0.5, "Tumor", "Normal")
rl_pred_class <- factor(rl_pred_class, levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))

# Matriz de confusao
rl_cm <- confusionMatrix(rl_pred_class, test_data$label, positive = "Tumor")
print(rl_cm)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction Normal Tumor
##     Normal      7    47
##     Tumor       1    34
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.4607          
##                  95% CI : (0.3544, 0.5696)
##     No Information Rate : 0.9101          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1               
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.0821          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 8.293e-11       
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.4198          
##             Specificity : 0.8750          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.9714          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.1296          
##              Prevalence : 0.9101          
##          Detection Rate : 0.3820          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.3933          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.6474          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : Tumor           
## 
rl_accuracy     <- rl_cm$overall["Accuracy"]
rl_precision    <- rl_cm$byClass["Precision"]
rl_recall       <- rl_cm$byClass["Recall"]
rl_f1           <- rl_cm$byClass["F1"]
rl_specificity  <- rl_cm$byClass["Specificity"]

cat("\n=== METRICAS DE DESEMPENHO - REGRESSAO LOGISTICA ===\n")
## 
## === METRICAS DE DESEMPENHO - REGRESSAO LOGISTICA ===
cat("Accuracy:   ", round(rl_accuracy,    4), "\n")
## Accuracy:    0.4607
cat("Precision:  ", round(rl_precision,   4), "\n")
## Precision:   0.9714
cat("Recall:     ", round(rl_recall,      4), "\n")
## Recall:      0.4198
cat("F1-Score:   ", round(rl_f1,          4), "\n")
## F1-Score:    0.5862
cat("Specificity:", round(rl_specificity, 4), "\n")
## Specificity: 0.875

Curva ROC e AUC

rl_roc_obj <- roc(test_data$label, rl_pred_prob,
               levels  = c("Normal", "Tumor"),
               direction = "<")

rl_auc_val <- auc(rl_roc_obj)
cat("AUC:", round(rl_auc_val, 4), "\n")
## AUC: 0.6767
# Grafico ROC
plot(rl_roc_obj,
     col  = "steelblue", lwd = 2,
     main = paste0("Curva ROC - Regressão Logistica (AUC = ", round(rl_auc_val, 3), ")"),
     print.auc = TRUE)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey50")

Importância dos Genes

# Extrair coeficientes do modelo
rl_coef <- summary(rl_model)$coefficients

# Criar dataframe de importância
rl_imp <- data.frame(
  gene = rownames(rl_coef),
  Estimate = rl_coef[, "Estimate"],
  P_value = rl_coef[, "Pr(>|z|)"]
)

# Remover intercepto
rl_imp <- rl_imp[-1, ]

# Criar métrica de importância
rl_imp$Importance <- abs(rl_imp$Estimate)

# Ordenar por importância
rl_imp <- rl_imp[order(rl_imp$Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# Top 20 genes
rl_top20_imp <- head(rl_imp, 20)

cat("Top 20 genes mais importantes:\n")
## Top 20 genes mais importantes:
print(rl_top20_imp)
##                                  gene  Estimate   P_value Importance
## ENSG00000211673.2   ENSG00000211673.2 -30.06966 0.9999795   30.06966
## ENSG00000234918.1   ENSG00000234918.1  27.88324 0.9999857   27.88324
## ENSG00000104332.12 ENSG00000104332.12  22.77745 0.9999906   22.77745
## ENSG00000196616.14 ENSG00000196616.14  21.42720 0.9999932   21.42720
## ENSG00000286208.1   ENSG00000286208.1 -20.31086 0.9999810   20.31086
## ENSG00000287900.1   ENSG00000287900.1  18.36483 0.9999864   18.36483
## ENSG00000124935.4   ENSG00000124935.4  17.86258 0.9999878   17.86258
## ENSG00000148513.18 ENSG00000148513.18 -16.99964 0.9999893   16.99964
## ENSG00000211945.2   ENSG00000211945.2  16.62388 0.9999883   16.62388
## ENSG00000211896.7   ENSG00000211896.7  16.28372 0.9999791   16.28372
## ENSG00000110484.7   ENSG00000110484.7 -15.98471 0.9999899   15.98471
## ENSG00000224559.2   ENSG00000224559.2  15.80416 0.9999823   15.80416
## ENSG00000186832.9   ENSG00000186832.9  14.67028 0.9999864   14.67028
## ENSG00000235687.9   ENSG00000235687.9 -14.26177 0.9999912   14.26177
## ENSG00000106541.12 ENSG00000106541.12  12.81825 0.9999890   12.81825
## ENSG00000184811.4   ENSG00000184811.4 -12.77376 0.9999926   12.77376
## ENSG00000162989.5   ENSG00000162989.5 -12.47516 0.9999865   12.47516
## ENSG00000211890.4   ENSG00000211890.4  11.69481 0.9999843   11.69481
## ENSG00000170323.9   ENSG00000170323.9 -11.52906 0.9999949   11.52906
## ENSG00000102243.13 ENSG00000102243.13 -10.88821 0.9999798   10.88821
ggplot(rl_top20_imp,
       aes(x = reorder(gene, Importance),
           y = Importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Importância dos Genes - Regressão Logística",
    x = "Gene",
    y = "Importance"
  )

Validação Cruzada (5-fold Cross-Validation)

set.seed(42)

# Configuração da validação cruzada
ctrl <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 5,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
  savePredictions = "final"
)

# Garantir classe positiva correta
exp_top$label <- relevel(exp_top$label, ref = "Normal")

# Treino do modelo com validação cruzada
rl_cv <- train(
  label ~ .,
  data      = exp_top,
  method    = "glm",
  family    = binomial,
  metric    = "ROC",
  trControl = ctrl
)
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning in predict.lm(object, newdata, se.fit, scale = 1, type = if (type == :
## prediction from rank-deficient fit; attr(*, "non-estim") has doubtful cases
## Warning: glm.fit: algorithm did not converge
# Resultados do modelo
print(rl_cv)
## Generalized Linear Model 
## 
## 300 samples
## 500 predictors
##   2 classes: 'Normal', 'Tumor' 
## 
## No pre-processing
## Resampling: Cross-Validated (5 fold) 
## Summary of sample sizes: 240, 239, 240, 241, 240 
## Resampling results:
## 
##   ROC        Sens       Spec     
##   0.5715769  0.5933333  0.5719192
# Métricas médias (CV)
cat("\n=== METRICAS MEDIA (5-fold CV) - REGRESSAO LOGISTICA ===\n")
## 
## === METRICAS MEDIA (5-fold CV) - REGRESSAO LOGISTICA ===
cat("AUC:          ", round(rl_cv$results$ROC,  4), "\n")
## AUC:           0.5716
cat("Sensibilidade:", round(rl_cv$results$Sens, 4), "\n")
## Sensibilidade: 0.5933
cat("Especificidade:", round(rl_cv$results$Spec, 4), "\n")
## Especificidade: 0.5719

Guardar Resultados para Comparação com Modelo 1

# Guardar métricas para comparação com o Modelo 1 (RF)
metricas_rl <- data.frame(
  Modelo      = "Regressao Logistica",
  Accuracy    = round(as.numeric(rl_accuracy),    4),
  Precision   = round(as.numeric(rl_precision),   4),
  Recall      = round(as.numeric(rl_recall),      4),
  F1          = round(as.numeric(rl_f1),          4),
  Specificity = round(as.numeric(rl_specificity), 4),
  AUC         = round(as.numeric(rl_auc_val),     4),
  AUC_CV      = round(rl_cv$results$ROC,      4)
)

# Exportar métricas
write.csv(metricas_rl,
          file = "metricas_modelo2_rl.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Exportar importância dos genes
write.csv(rl_imp,
          file = "importancia_genes_rl.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# Exportar probabilidades previstas
write.csv(data.frame(rl_pred_prob),
          file = "predicoes_rl.csv",
          row.names = TRUE)

cat("Ficheiros exportados com sucesso.\n")
## Ficheiros exportados com sucesso.
print(metricas_rl)
##                Modelo Accuracy Precision Recall     F1 Specificity    AUC
## 1 Regressao Logistica   0.4607    0.9714 0.4198 0.5862       0.875 0.6767
##   AUC_CV
## 1 0.5716

Comparação do modelo de Random Forest com o modelo de Regressão logistica

comparacao_modelos <- rbind(metricas_rf, metricas_rl)

print(comparacao_modelos)
##                Modelo Accuracy Precision Recall     F1 Specificity    AUC
## 1       Random Forest   1.0000    1.0000 1.0000 1.0000       1.000 1.0000
## 2 Regressao Logistica   0.4607    0.9714 0.4198 0.5862       0.875 0.6767
##   AUC_CV
## 1 0.9988
## 2 0.5716
# Converter para formato adequado
comp_long <- melt(comparacao_modelos, id.vars = "Modelo")

# Gráfico comparativo
ggplot(comp_long, aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = Modelo)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparação Final dos Modelos",
    x = "Métrica",
    y = "Valor"
  )

# ROC - Random Forest
rf_roc <- roc(
  response  = test_data$label,
  predictor = rf_pred_prob,
  levels    = c("Normal", "Tumor"),
  direction = "<"
)

# ROC - Regressão Logística
rl_roc <- roc(
  response  = test_data$label,
  predictor = rl_pred_prob,
  levels    = c("Normal", "Tumor"),
  direction = "<"
)

# Plot base (Random Forest)
plot(
  rf_roc,
  col  = "darkgreen",
  lwd  = 2,
  main = "Curvas ROC: Random Forest vs Regressao Logistica"
)

# Adicionar Regressão Logística
plot(
  rl_roc,
  col  = "darkred",
  lwd  = 2,
  add  = TRUE
)

# Linha de referência
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey")

# Legenda com AUC
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c(
    paste0("Random Forest (AUC = ", metricas_rf$AUC, ")"),
    paste0("Regressao Logistica (AUC = ", metricas_rl$AUC, ")")
  ),
  col = c("darkgreen", "darkred"),
  lwd = 2
)

Análise dos resultados

top5_rf_genes <- c("ENSG00000123500.10", "ENSG00000161649.13", 
                   "ENSG00000168079.17", "ENSG00000101938.15", 
                   "ENSG00000065618.21")


medias_top5 <- rowMeans(logcounts[top5_rf_genes, ])


df_top5_exp <- data.frame(
  Gene = names(medias_top5),
  MeanExpression = medias_top5
)


ggplot(df_top5_exp, aes(x = reorder(Gene, MeanExpression), y = MeanExpression)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylab("Média de expressão (logcounts)") +
  xlab("Gene") +
  ggtitle("Expressão Média dos Top 5 Genes mais importantes (Random Forest)")

top5_rf_genes <- c("ENSG00000123500.10", "ENSG00000161649.13", 
                   "ENSG00000168079.17", "ENSG00000101938.15", 
                   "ENSG00000065618.21")

matriz_heatmap <- t(exp_top[, top5_rf_genes])

anotacao_amostras <- data.frame(Condicao = exp_top$label)
rownames(anotacao_amostras) <- rownames(exp_top)

pheatmap(matriz_heatmap, 
         annotation_col = anotacao_amostras,
         scale = "row", # Escala por gene para ver bem a diferença de cores
         show_colnames = FALSE, 
         cluster_cols = TRUE,
         main = "Expressão dos 5 Principais Genes (Random Forest)")

Na comparação entre o Random Forest e a Regressão Logística, é possível observar que o Random Forest supera o outro modelo em todas as métricas analisadas. O algoritmo identificou os genes ENSG00000123500.10, ENSG00000161649.13, ENSG00000168079.17, ENSG00000101938.15 e ENSG00000065618.21. Quanto à sua expressão, estes genes não são os mais expressos. Comparando aos genes mais expressos, analisados na fase 1, que chegavam aos 13 logcounts, o gene considerado mais importante pelo modelo Random Forest chegou apenas aos 6 logcounts. Apesar desta discrepância, o valor destes genes provém da sua enorme capacidade de distinção entre amostras de tumor e tecido normal, representando um painel de biomarcadores clinicamente relevantes para o carcinoma da mama.
